channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: What to know about the Taurid meteor shower
item.description: With the Taurid meteor shower now hitting the night skies worldwide, look for what could be a celestial treat. You might see shooting stars, and maybe even fireballs, the biggest and brightest meteors. As the full moon begins to wane after Nov.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: San Francisco’s mayor-elect picks Sam Altman to co-chair transition team
item.description: San Francisco Mayor-elect Daniel Lurie has picked OpenAI CEO Sam Altman to help run his transition team as he prepares to take office in January, Lurie said.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: How ATO as code can reduce this compliance burden
item.description: OPM’s Dan Jacobs and stackArmor’s Gaurav “GP” Pal led an ACT-IAC team to develop a maturity model to help implement authority to operate as code. The post How ATO as code can reduce this compliance burden first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Amazon and Elon Musk's SpaceX challenge labor agency's constitutionality in federal court
item.description: Attorneys for Amazon and Elon Musk’s SpaceX have argued in a federal appeals court that the National Labor Relations Board’s structure is unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Data-driven startup Aggie uses AI to manage small business social media
item.description: A new, AI-powered platform called Aggie wants to manage your brand’s social media. Aggie is a product of Audience Genomics, which has since 2018 offered audience data analytics to big brands like Universal Studios, AMC Television, and Fenty Beauty.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: Brendan Carr Appointed as New FCC Chairman: Implications and Controversies
item.description: Brendan Carr's appointment as new FCC chairman raises concerns about his ties to tech billionaire Elon Musk and plans for overturning Section 230.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Company affiliated with Alex Jones seeks to disqualify The Onion's auction bid on Infowars
item.description: A company affiliated with conspiracy theorist Alex Jones asked a federal judge on Monday to disqualify a bid by the satirical news outlet The Onion to buy Jones' Infowars at a bankruptcy auction, alleging fraud and collusion.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge tosses Huckabee lawsuit against Meta over ads suggesting he endorsed marijuana gummies
item.description: WILMINGTON, Del. (AP) — A federal judge in Delaware on Monday dismissed a lawsuit filed by former Arkansas Gov. Mike Huckabee against social media giant Meta over advertisements using his name and image to sell CBD products.
item.dc:creator: RANDALL CHASE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Google might resurrect Pixel laptop as a high-end Chromebook to take on MacBook Pro and Surface Laptop
item.description: Rumor has it that Google is bringing back its laptop hardware, which would please a lot of the plentiful Pixelbook fans out there.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: How Trump’s appointment of Brendan Carr as FCC chairman will affect enterprises
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has announced Brendan Carr as the next head of the US Federal Communications Commission (FCC), the organization responsible for setting the regulations governing telecoms, broadband, satellite communications and broadcasting in the US.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Trump team is seeking to ease US Rules for self-driving cars
item.description: Current federal rules pose significant roadblocks for companies looking to launch vehicles without steering wheels or pedals in large quantities, which Tesla intends to do.
item.dc:creator: Tribune News Service, The Mercury News

item.title: 1 in 5 get news from social media influencers: Pew poll
item.description: About 1 in 5 Americans say they get their news from social media influencers, according to a new poll from the Pew Research Center. Of the 21 percent of adults who said they get their news from influencers on social media, the split was roughly even across parties.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, The Hill

item.title: AWS' Shift in Open Source Strategy: From Contributions to Customer-Centric Approach
item.description: AWS has been slowly shifting its open source strategy from focusing on contributions to customer-centric approach, prioritizing ease of use and excellent support over developer involvement.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 14 great preprocessors for developers who love to code
item.description: As much as we love them, programming languages can often feel like straitjackets. They’re a complex bundle of syntactic rules, and if we break them–even once–the compiler starts screaming out error messages.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Sólo el 21% de las empresas se apoya en la tecnología para diseñar estrategias de sostenibilidad
item.description: A pesar de que el 84% de las organizaciones considera de importancia “estratégica” sus objetivos de sostenibilidad, sólo el 21% de ellas se apoya en la tecnología para reducir su huella medioambiental y configurar su estrategia global en este ámbito.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: CIO Middle East and IDC hosts the inaugural CIO100 Awards, celebrating the region’s top 100 tech leaders
item.description: In a landmark event for the Middle East’s technology sector, Foundry and IDC have officially launched the inaugural CIO100 Middle East Awards in Dubai, spotlighting the region’s most influential and visionary CIOs and tech leaders.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The Joe Rogan Experience: A Missing Piece in the 2020 Presidential Campaign
item.description: The role of podcasting, particularly The Joe Rogan Experience, in shaping public opinion during the 2020 presidential campaign.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Bien-Kahn, Fast Company

item.title: How a wrong Google search can compromise your data and bring law enforcement calling
item.description: Wrong Google searches can not only compromise your device and personal data. They can also bring law enforcement to your doorstep. Hackers are targeting Google searches.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dude Perfect went from YouTube to raising $100M. Here’s how its new CEO wants to grow the brand
item.description: Over the past 15 years, sports and entertainment company Dude Perfect—which started as a YouTube channel where five best friends posted videos of trick shots in different sports—has grown into a media juggernaut with attendant cruises, toy lines, and TV deals.
item.dc:creator: Yasmin Gagne, Fast Company

item.title: How to make your VPN faster
item.description: Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson explains how a VPN — virtual private network — works and how running it can slow down the operation of your device.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lawsuit alleging Elon Musk rigged Dogecoin is dropped
item.description: A lawsuit accusing Elon Musk of rigging Dogecoin is ending. Investors in the cryptocurrency who said the world’s richest person and his electric car company Tesla committed fraud and insider trading are withdrawing their appeal from an Aug. 29 dismissal of their case.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft and Adobe Software Updates: Critical Fixes Released
item.description: Learn about recent software updates from Microsoft, Adobe, and other vendors, including critical fixes for Windows, Microsoft Edge, and SQL Server Native Client.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft’s anticompetitive behavior weakens its customers’ cybersecurity
item.description: If left unchecked, Microsoft’s practices will become the industry standard, clearing the path for other providers to harm customers and weaken their defenses. The post Microsoft’s anticompetitive behavior weakens its customers’ cybersecurity first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: JetBrains IDEs ease debugging for Kubernetes apps
item.description: JetBrains IDEs, with new 2024.3 edition releases, now provide a logical code structure view alongside the familiar physical code structure view in the Structure tool window.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Musk: DOGE will be 'tedious work' with 'zero' compensation
item.description: Elon Musk said in a post on the social platform X on Thursday that the brand new Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) — an advisory group under President-elect Trump focused on slashing government costs and restructuring federal agencies — will require "tedious work" with "zero" compensation.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Solidigm unveils 122TB data center SSD for AI workloads
item.description: Solidigm, a provider of NAND flash solid-state drives, and memory giant Micron have each introduced very high-capacity SSD drives meant for enterprise data centers, particularly in AI use cases. Traditional hard disk drives have maintained their viability because of capacity advantages over SSDs.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: In Cyprus, Ukrainians learn how to dispose of landmines that kill and maim hundreds
item.description: NICOSIA, Cyprus (AP) — In a Cypriot National Guard camp, Ukrainians are being trained on how to identify, locate and dispose of landmines and other unexploded munitions that litter huge swaths of their country, killing and maiming hundreds of people, including children.
item.dc:creator: MENELAOS HADJICOSTIS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: One Tech Tip: Replacing passwords with passkeys for an easier login experience
item.description: LONDON (AP) — If you're tired of memorizing passwords, then give passkeys a try. You might have noticed that many online services are now offering the option of using passkeys, a digital authentication method touted as an easier and more secure way to log in.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Solar-powered aircraft achieves groundbreaking 22-hour autonomous flight
item.description: Skydweller is the world's largest unmanned solar-powered aircraft that can stay airborne for weeks, and even months, without refueling.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NASA Establishes New Class of Astrophysics Missions, Selects Studies
item.description: NASA selects two proposals for missions to observe X-ray and far-infrared wavelengths of light from space for additional review The post NASA Establishes New Class of Astrophysics Missions, Selects Studies first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Eric White, Federal News Network

item.title: Elon Musk says he and Trump have 'mandate to delete' regulations. Ethics laws could limit Musk role
item.description: In picking billionaire Elon Musk to be “our cost cutter” for the U.S. government, President-elect Donald Trump won't be the first American president to empower a business tycoon to look for ways to dramatically cut federal regulations. President Ronald Reagan tapped J.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and TOM KRISHER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: CISA’s SILENTSHIELD assessment requires urgent measures
item.description: The SILENTSHIELD red team assessment has highlighted the urgent need for enhanced cyber resilience within federal agencies. The post CISA’s SILENTSHIELD assessment requires urgent measures first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: VMware makes Workstation and Fusion free for everyone
item.description: VMware this week announced that the virtualization company’s hypervisor products, VMware Workstation and Fusion, will be completely free for both personal and commercial users, as well as for educational purposes.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Oops, here’s how to edit and unsend a message on iPhone before it’s too late
item.description: You can unsend or edit your iMessages with the latest iOS updates. Kurt the CyberGuy explains how you can save yourself some potential embarrassment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Agile Manifesto was ahead of its time
item.description: Last week I went on a short rant about scrum, and how it isn’t agile and is, well, dumb. It occurred to me that one of the obstacles to truly being agile has been the limits of software technology. The way we had to build and deliver software didn’t lend itself to flexibility.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Visual Studio 17.12 brings C++, Copilot enhancements
item.description: Visual Studio 2022 17.12, the latest version of Microsoft’s signature integrated development environment (IDE), is now generally available, with improvements ranging from productivity enhancements for C++ developers to integrated AI variable inspection in the GitHub Copilot AI-powered programming as
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Chinese hackers target Tibetan websites in malware attack, cybersecurity group says
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A hacking group that is believed to be Chinese state-sponsored has compromised two websites with ties to the Tibetan community in an attack meant to install malware on users' computers, according to findings released Wednesday by a private cybersecurity firm.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING, Associated Press, Fox 5
